eventMetadata.context_description: French President Emmanuel Macron continues to navigate France's fragmented parliament and repeated government instability, including multiple prime minister resignations and no-confidence challenges in 2025 and 2026, while consistently rejecting early exit calls. His second and final five-year term, limited by constitutional rules, runs until the 2027 presidential election. In late April 2026 Macron publicly confirmed he would step away from politics entirely at that point, stating he had not been involved before entering office and would not remain afterward. These dynamics, alongside scheduled legislative maneuvers and potential snap elections, inform trader assessments of any departure before the term's natural conclusion.

eventMetadata.context_description: The UK’s next general election remains scheduled no later than August 2029 under the five-year parliamentary term that began after the July 2024 vote. The prime minister holds the power to dissolve Parliament and set an earlier date at any time, but recent poor results for Labour in the May 2026 local and devolved elections have fueled internal party pressure on Keir Starmer without shifting the timeline toward a snap contest this summer. Traders are watching for any sign of a leadership challenge or major policy reversal that could force an earlier ballot, yet the current parliamentary arithmetic and lack of an immediate crisis make a June 2026 announcement unlikely.

title: China x India military clash by...?
description: This is a market on the likelihood of a military clash between China and India occurring by December 31.

markets.question: China x India military clash by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of China (People's Republic of China) and India between January 29, and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between Chinese and Indian military forces. Hand-to-hand combat with the use of melee weapons between military personnel, will qualify. For example the 2020 Galwan Valley clash and the 2022 Yangtse clash would both count.

Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the China Coast Guard (CCG) is part of the military, however the Indian Coast Guard (ICG) is not.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing diplomatic and military talks between India and China continue to shape trader assessments of a potential clash along the disputed Line of Actual Control. The two sides held their 23rd Corps Commander-level meeting in October 2025 at the Chushul-Moldo border point, reaffirming use of existing mechanisms to address ground issues and maintain stability following the 2024 patrolling agreement that resolved earlier disengagement challenges in Ladakh. Both governments have emphasized trade and security cooperation over border friction, though Chinese military upgrades for high-altitude operations and statements designating Arunachal Pradesh a core interest sustain underlying risks. Scheduled high-level engagements and routine patrols remain key near-term factors that could influence escalation probabilities.

title: NATO/EU troops fighting in Ukraine by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if active military personnel officially affiliated with any NATO or EU country enter Ukraine for combat-related military purposes directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, they must be 1) officially acknowledged as active military by the NATO or EU entity or member state they are affiliated with; 2) be publicly acknowledged by NATO or an EU-affiliated entity to have entered Ukraine for a combat-related military purpose directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia.

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution they need be active duty and acknowledged as described above. Participation in a combat role is necessary for this market to resolve to "Yes" (e.g. military personnel providing training or intelligence support would not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, however drone pilots or infantry directly attacking Russian troops, or soldiers targeting and downing missiles from Ukrainian soil would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO, the EU, or member states of either entity, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent diplomatic coordination between NATO and the EU on April 23, 2026, emphasized sustained military aid and training support for Ukraine without committing alliance forces to direct combat operations inside the country. Ukrainian units continue scheduled basic training programs in three EU member states backed by four NATO partners throughout 2026, while Ukrainian drone teams participated as opposing forces in Sweden’s Aurora 2026 exercise through mid-May, highlighting tactical exchanges rather than operational deployments. U.S. officials have separately warned that any ceasefire could redirect Russian pressure toward NATO’s eastern flank, prompting ongoing alliance discussions on readiness and rearmament. No official announcements have confirmed plans for NATO or EU combat troops to engage in fighting, though future joint meetings scheduled for September and December could address expanded assistance measures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent developments in UK politics have intensified pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer following Labour’s heavy losses in May local and devolved elections, triggering multiple cabinet resignations including Health Secretary Wes Streeting and public calls from nearly 100 Labour MPs for his resignation or an explicit departure timetable. Starmer has publicly resisted stepping down while privately acknowledging the need for an orderly transition, with allies urging him to delay any announcement until after the Makerfield by-election on June 18. Low personal approval ratings and internal party divisions over policy direction have positioned him as an interim figure in the eyes of many MPs, though no formal leadership contest has been launched. These factors, combined with the UK’s parliamentary system allowing rapid leadership changes through party mechanisms, shape trader assessments of when Starmer might cease to hold office.

title: Ukraine recognizes Russian sovereignty over its territory by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a deal in which Ukraine agrees to formally recognize Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory is reached between the Russian Federation and Ukraine by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The deal must be a publicly announced mutual agreement. 

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995, “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia, which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement, is an example of a qualifying agreement.

An official unilateral pledge by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution even if not part of an agreement with Russia.

The April 19, 2013, Brussels Agreement between Serbia and Kosovo, in which Serbia recognized Kosovo’s de facto administration but did not formally grant de jure recognition, would not be considered a qualifying agreement under this market, as this market requires formal recognition of sovereignty rather than acknowledgment of administrative control.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by Ukraine and/or the Russian Federation, however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial, declared after a hung jury on the Jessica Mann charge, has sharply elevated trader consensus around “No Prison Time” at 82.5% implied probability. The 74-year-old former producer’s ongoing California 16-year sentence remains under appeal, while health complications including heart issues have repeatedly interrupted proceedings at Rikers Island. These factors, alongside the weakened New York case and historical patterns of reduced sentences for elderly defendants, have shifted momentum away from longer terms like 20–30 years. Key upcoming catalysts include prosecutorial decisions on a potential fourth trial and California appeal outcomes, both of which could further compress any additional incarceration timeline.

title: Will OpenAI launch a consumer hardware product by...?
description: This is a market on whether OpenAI will release a consumer hardware product by the specified date.

markets.question: Will OpenAI launch a new consumer hardware product in 2025?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI publicly announces and launches a new consumer hardware product by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

A "consumer hardware product" is defined as a physical device intended for direct use by individual consumers, rather than enterprise or developer tools. The product must be newly introduced and not a rebrand, update, or iteration of any previously released device.

Examples that would qualify include an AI-powered wearable, smart home assistant, or augmented reality device. Developer tools, AI chips, or servers marketed exclusively for enterprise use would not qualify.

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpenAI’s entry into consumer hardware hinges on its 2025 acquisition of Jony Ive’s io Products and ongoing integration of large language models into a new category of AI devices. Recent statements from executives confirm the company is targeting an unveil of its first product—a likely screenless smart speaker with local AI processing—in the second half of 2026, though court filings and supply-chain reports point to possible shipping delays until early 2027 amid challenges with on-device inference, privacy safeguards, and user-interface design. These developments, set against broader competition from Apple’s ecosystem and Google’s hardware efforts, shape trader views on whether OpenAI can meet aggressive internal timelines for a meaningful consumer launch.

title: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by...?
description: This is a market on the likelihood of Russia capturing Kostyantynivka by a specific date.

markets.question: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by August 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Kostyantynivka railroad station located on Pravoberezhna vulytsia by August 31, 2025 at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by August 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Train Station Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka3.jpg

Train Station Location in Kostyantynivka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka2.jpg

Kostyantynivka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka1.jpg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/hZ6ch5jVRvkNFY317

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Kostyantynivka railroad station located on Pravoberezhna vulytsia by April 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Train Station Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka3.jpg

Train Station Location in Kostyantynivka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka2.jpg

Kostyantynivka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka1.jpg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/hZ6ch5jVRvkNFY317

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have intensified ground assaults and small-unit infiltration missions around Kostyantynivka in Donetsk Oblast since late April 2026, securing limited gains on the eastern and southwestern outskirts while claiming control of nearby settlements such as Novodmytrivka. Ukrainian commanders report repelling dozens of daily probes through coordinated drone strikes and defensive rotations, with independent assessments confirming Russian presence in under 10 percent of the city and no confirmed breakthroughs into central areas. The slow pace of advances stems from high attrition, logistical strains, and Ukrainian counter-moves that have stalled earlier momentum from the 2025 autumn push. Traders weigh these frontline dynamics against the broader Russian priority on the Kramatorsk-Druzhkivka defensive belt, where further consolidation could influence timelines for any urban capture.

title: Spain snap election called by...?
description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s minority government has sustained legislative stability through alliances with regional parties such as Junts and ERC, reducing immediate pressure for an early dissolution of the Cortes Generales. In late April 2026 Sánchez publicly ruled out a snap election, underscoring plans to complete eight years of progressive governance ahead of the constitutionally scheduled general election no later than August 2027. Recent regional ballots in Aragón and Extremadura delivered setbacks for the PSOE while boosting the far-right Vox, yet national polling continues to show the Socialists competitive and the opposition Popular Party unable to force a moción de censura or budget crisis. Traders view these factors, alongside the absence of major coalition fractures or corruption triggers sufficient to shift the balance, as supporting a strong consensus against a 2026 snap vote.

title: US x Russia military clash by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Russia between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between U.S. and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify. 

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage. 

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Diplomatic initiatives by the Trump administration, including a mediated three-day ceasefire in the Russia-Ukraine conflict from May 9 to 11, 2026, and resumed high-level U.S.-Russia military-to-military talks since February, have shaped trader assessments of low near-term risk for direct confrontation. These efforts, alongside prisoner exchanges and ongoing negotiations, reflect efforts to manage escalation in the broader Ukraine theater while NATO maintains enhanced eastern flank deployments. Persistent gray-zone provocations and differing strategic priorities between Washington and Moscow continue to warrant monitoring, though scheduled diplomatic channels and ceasefire verification processes through June could further influence probabilities ahead of any potential summer flashpoints.

title: Will Russia invade a NATO country by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Russia commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of any NATO country between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by NATO member states or Russia as of May 28, 2025, will be considered the sovereign territory of those countries.

Hybrid or “grey zone” operations conducted by unmarked personnel will qualify if they are confirmed to be Russian operatives and are intended to establish de facto control over territory, as occurred in Crimea (2014) or Donbas (2014–2022).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent assessments from Western intelligence agencies highlight Russia's ongoing military reconstitution after its Ukraine campaign, with a Dutch military intelligence report in April 2026 warning that Moscow could develop sufficient forces for a limited regional challenge to NATO within a year of any ceasefire there. Such an action would likely seek to exploit alliance divisions through targeted gains or hybrid pressure rather than full confrontation. NATO has responded with enhanced eastern-flank exercises, including recent Swedish-led drills on Gotland testing responses to sabotage and airspace probes, while reported drone incidents near Baltic borders underscore persistent tensions. Ongoing diplomatic channels between Washington and Moscow, alongside the unresolved Ukraine conflict, continue to shape trader views on the timing and likelihood of any direct incursion.

title: Trump eliminates capital gains tax on crypto by ___?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump signs a bill into law or performs any executive action changing US tax law to eliminate capital gains tax on any cryptocurrency assets by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Such a bill must apply to US taxpaying persons in general; if a law applies only to specific companies or institutions, it will not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market.

If capital gains is eliminated only for specific types or classes of crypto assets (e.g., only Bitcoin or Ethereum, only American issued cryptocurrencies, or only NFTs), it will still count toward a "Yes" resolution for this market.

If the bill is signed into law, or executive action is instituted before the resolution date, it will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of when it takes effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US federal government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing US-mediated security talks between Israel and Syria, including January 2026 meetings in Paris that established a joint communication mechanism for intelligence sharing and de-escalation, represent the primary driver shaping trader views on normalization timelines. These discussions focus on border stability, reactivation of the 1974 disengagement framework, and preventing Iranian or proxy threats in southern Syria, yet Syrian officials continue to condition any broader agreement on Israeli withdrawal from areas advanced after the December 2024 fall of the Assad regime. Israel, for its part, maintains core requirements around Golan Heights sovereignty and minority protections while exploring limited security pacts short of full diplomatic recognition or Abraham Accords-style ties. With territorial disputes and differing red lines still unresolved, near-term normalization faces significant procedural and political hurdles despite the shift from outright hostility to structured engagement.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2025-26 NHL playoffs have tightened the 2026 Stanley Cup race around the Carolina Hurricanes and Colorado Avalanche, both entering as regular-season standouts with perfect records through their opening series. Carolina extended an undefeated postseason run to eight straight wins en route to the Eastern Conference Finals after sweeping the Ottawa Senators and Philadelphia Flyers, showcasing depth across all lines and strong goaltending. Colorado, the league’s oldest roster, has mirrored that momentum in the West while advancing toward a potential clash with the Vegas Golden Knights. These recent surges, combined with proven regular-season form and favorable matchups against younger or less experienced opponents, underpin trader consensus around the top two clubs. The lower-priced Vegas Golden Knights, Montreal Canadiens, and Buffalo Sabres trail due to tougher paths and less consistent recent results, keeping the overall field competitive into the conference finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's status as defending champions with the NBA's best regular-season record and an undefeated playoff run through two rounds has solidified trader consensus around their 58.5% implied probability for the 2026 title. Their depth at every position, including standout performances from Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Chet Holmgren, combined with a favorable path in the Western Conference Finals against the San Antonio Spurs, reinforces this edge. The Spurs sit at 24.5% on the strength of Victor Wembanyama's emergence and recent series wins, positioning them as the primary threat to a Thunder repeat. In the East, the New York Knicks at 13.0% benefit from their semifinal sweep, while the Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers remain long shots at under 4% amid their ongoing seven-game series.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France leads the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market at 18.6% implied probability, narrowly ahead of Spain, as traders weigh recent form and squad depth across the expanded 48-team field. Spain’s long unbeaten streak in 90-minute matches and strong European Championship pedigree keep it tightly bunched at 16.8%, while England, Brazil, and Argentina sit just behind on the strength of established attacking talent and qualification results. Recent international friendlies have highlighted France’s attacking options and Spain’s possession control, yet injury concerns for key players like Lamine Yamal and ongoing squad finalization across contenders sustain the competitive balance. The wisdom of crowds reflected in these probabilities underscores how evenly matched the leading nations remain with one month until kickoff.
